content.boundary: Portuguese and Italian evidence on closure-mitigation pairing is documented, but the precise Hungarian cost data are absent, and TEIP evidence shows compensatory measures do not resolve equity gaps, so overall net effect is contested.
content.change_lever: Portugal's 2005/2006 school network reorganization paired the closure of 1,418 schools with construction of 800 larger, better-equipped 'centros escolares,' demonstrating that closure can be paired with matched receiving-site investment.; Post-closure transportation costs have been cited in Central European contexts in a range of roughly 800-1,500 EUR per pupil per year, though this precise figure has not been validated against Hungarian route lengths, occupancy rates, or regional terrain, and often offsets part of the projected consolidation savings.
content.name: Consolidation with mitigation (closure as default, compensatory measures)
content.system_problem: Close low-enrollment village schools and reassign pupils to a consolidated center school, paired with statutory transport guarantees, targeted support (TEIP-style), and transparent cost/consultation rules to offset travel and equity harms.
